chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI C.P. No. D-5744 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES 1. For hearing of CMA No. 25499 of 2024. 2. For hearing of main case. 19-11-2024 Mr. Muhammad Saad Shfiq Siddiqui, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. Khaleeq Ahmed, Deputy Attorney General for Pakistan. ********** Mr. Muhammad Khali Dogar, Advocate under takes to file Vakalatnama on behalf of Respondent No.2. Valuation Ruling 1681/2022, dated 22.17.2022, issued under Section 25A of the Customs Act in respect of “Electrolyte Tin Plate and Tin Free Sheet” [subject goods] was set-aside by the Customs Appellate Tribunal vide judgment dated 01.02.2024 passed on Customs Appeal No.K-1912/2023 along with other appeals. The Customs department preferred SCRAs No.100 to 109 of 2024 before the High Court, wherein judgment of the Customs Appellate Tribunal remains suspended. In C.P.No.D-843/2024, a learned Division Bench of this Court observed that suspension of the Tribunal’s judgment in the SCRAs did not ipso facto revive the Valuation Ruling, and therefore, passed an order for the provisional release of subject goods imported meanwhile. The Petitioner prays for the same relief. Counsel for the Customs department does not oppose. In the given circumstances, where the manner of assessment of duties and taxes on the subject goods remains unresolved, if the disputed amount of duty and taxes on the consignment covered by GD mentioned in paragraph 5 of the petition viz. KAPW-IB-69125 dated 02-11-2024 is secured by the Petitioner with the Nazir of this Court by pay-order or bank guarantee, the Nazir shall issue an appropriate certificate to the Petitioner, and on presenting the same

In the given circumstances, where the manner of assessment of duties and taxes on the subject goods remains unresolved, if the disputed amount of duty and taxes on the consignment covered by GD mentioned in paragraph 5 of the petition viz. KAPW-IB-69125 dated 02-11-2024 is secured by the Petitioner with the Nazir of this Court by pay-order or bank guarantee, the Nazir shall issue an appropriate certificate to the Petitioner, and on presenting the same CPD No. 5744 of 2024 (VR) before the concerned Collectorate/Department, the latter shall release the aforesaid consignment forthwith. Where the security is a pay-order, Nazir shall invest the same in a government profit bearing instrument as per Rules. The Nazir’s fee is fixed as Rs.10,000/- per certificate. The above is of course subject to any further order passed in the connected SCRAs. Counsel for the Petitioner does not press any other prayer. Therefore, petition is disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE *PA/SADAM
